mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as long as FEDFUNDS remains in the easing regime, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% as of 2008-11-15 indicates extreme easing
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS < 1% are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10 over any 2-year rolling window), or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 6.5% in a single month, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as market reprices recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% as of 2008-11-15 indicates elevated unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 6.5%, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 800 thousand units, indicating synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand. This joint deterioration signals recession deepening and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 800 thousand units AND RSAFS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 791.0 thousand as of 2008-10-15 indicates housing collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no statistically significant incremental predictive power beyond individual series (interaction coefficient p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 106.0 index points, indicating contraction in manufacturing and supply-side activity. This signals reduced corporate profitability and capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.1173 as of 2008-11-15 near critical threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO < 106.0 are not significantly negative (mean return > -1.0% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines by more than 500 thousand persons month-over-month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously rises, creating a synchronized labor market shock. This dual deterioration triggers equity selloffs and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 500 thousand AND UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136,167 thousand and UNRATE at 6.7% indicate labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (interaction p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 217.0 index points while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 1%, indicating deflation risk and monetary policy accommodation. This regime of low inflation and low rates supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 216.71 and FEDFUNDS at 0.39% indicate deflationary monetary easing
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in this regime are not significantly positive (mean < 0.3% or t-test p > 0.15), or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth trajectory shows contraction or near-zero growth, indicating recession. This fundamental demand weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as market reprices earnings and growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 shows quarter-over-quarter decline or growth < 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,712.3 billion (2017 dollars) as of 2008-07-15 in recession period
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 contraction are not significantly negative (mean > -0.5% or t-test p > 0.12), or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously contracts, indicating synchronized supply and labor market deterioration. This dual shock signals severe recession and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as both supply capacity and demand-side employment collapse.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.0 points month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 300 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:Both INDPRO and PAYEMS show contraction signals in late 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (interaction p > 0.18).
